---
output: github_document
---

<!-- README.md is generated from README.Rmd. Please edit that file -->

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>",
  fig.path = "man/figures/README-",
  out.width = "100%"
)
```

# fpaR

<!-- badges: start -->
<!-- badges: end -->

This is a collection of business intelligence tools to help with common data queries and insights. 

Aim of the package is grow over time to include

1) segmentation strategies
2) time intelligence functions
3) applied statitical techniques for variance analysis

## Installation

You can install the development version of fpaR from [GitHub](https://github.com/) with:

``` r
# install.packages("devtools")
devtools::install_github("alejandrohagan/fpaR")
```

This is correctly under development and is in early stages!

Current functions:

**make_segmentation()** will use kmeans and umap to create a segmentation
**count_plus** will replicate the handy dplyr::count() function
